title: 命案目睹记
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\136-命案目睹记-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Harold, reflecting on the precarious financial state of his company, visits his office to assess its situation. He contemplates the death of Alfred, noting that Alfred's death increased Harold's share of their irritable grandfather's inheritance. Harold feels a sense of relief regarding his own health, though he keeps his arsenic poisoning a secret from his wife, Alice.

Upon returning home, Harold is greeted by his butler, Darwin, and finds Alice has returned from Saint Raphael. Harold views Alice as a well-mannered but mediocre woman; they share a marriage characterized by a lack of communication and mutual interest. During dinner, Alice mentions a news report about food poisoning, and they discuss the discovery of a French woman's body in the Rutherford Manor warehouse. Harold notes that the police have not yet found the killer.

Later, Harold finds a small package in the hall containing a pillbox with a note from a Blakelamp pharmacy, sent at the request of Dr. Campbell. The label instructs him to take two pills at bedtime. Harold becomes suspicious and worried because he recalls Dr. Campbell telling him he no longer needed to take such medication. When he questions Alice, she gently suggests he may have misunderstood the doctor. As Harold sits by the fireplace, he reflects on his loveless marriage, their lack of children, and the controversial will of his grandfather, which has left the family members dissatisfied. The combination of these thoughts leaves Harold feeling uncomfortable, tired, and dizzy.
